response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role demands deep hardware/SoC expertise — performance architecture for AI accelerators, SoC/IP modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, and GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture. The resume shows none of these specialized hardware competencies, no relevant graduate degree, and no AI accelerator or chip-design background. Mismatch is fundamental, not bridgeable through onboarding.
strengths: Long track record of shipping production software across diverse stacks
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang, C/C++ at National Instruments, and embedded work
Demonstrated engineering rigor: TDD, CI/CD, performance-sensitive low-latency services at Sociomantic
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI accelerator experience
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or GPU architecture background
Educational path (BS only) without the years of specialized hardware experience the role requires

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling & AI accelerator expertise
